summary: Shahed attack drones are repeatedly reported over Kyiv and surrounding regions, while Russian drone strikes hit Zaporizhzhia and Sumy causing casualties and infrastructure damage. Russian forces intensify pressure around Pokrovsk and Dobropil, employing large numbers of various drone types. A reported Iskander‑M ballistic missile threat from Voronezh adds a new strategic risk.
key_points: Shahed‑type attack drones are observed heading toward Kyiv from Barishivka, Borispol and multiple launches over the city (Source: @kpszsu/65492, @kpszsu/65500, @mon1tor_ua/67867).
Shahed drones are reported moving toward Poltava from Pavlenky and Ocherka, indicating pressure on central Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67858, @kpszsu/65493).
Shahed drones are advancing toward Kharkiv from Lebedyn, Okhtyrka and Ocherka, suggesting coordinated strikes on the northeast (Source: @kpszsu/65488, @kpszsu/65493).

Russian Shahed attacks on Zaporizhzhia killed one civilian, injured seven, and heavily damaged residential blocks, an office centre and the Zaporizhzhia National University (Source: @UkraineNow/81983).
A Russian drone strike on an equestrian school in Sumy destroyed a stable and killed horses, though no human casualties were reported (Source: @UkraineNow/81984).
Russian forces are intensifying pressure around Pokrovsk, Mirnograd and Dobropil, employing large numbers of FPV, Lancet, Molniya, Shahed and other drones (Source: @DeepStateUA/23584).
A threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Voronezh region is reported, raising the risk of long‑range strikes (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67830).
Ukrainian drones damaged the main processing unit of the largest Moscow oil refinery, halting operations (Source: @UkraineNow/82002).

sections.executive_summary: Shahed attack drones are repeatedly observed over Kyiv and heading toward Poltava and Kharkiv, indicating sustained aerial pressure on central and northeastern Ukraine. Russian drone strikes have caused civilian casualties and significant infrastructure damage in Zaporizhzhia and Sumy. An emerging Iskander‑M ballistic missile threat from Voronezh adds a new strategic dimension to the conflict.

points: Ukrainian Shahed‑type attack drones were sighted moving from Barishivka toward Kyiv, from Borispol toward Kyiv, and multiple Shaheds were reported over Kyiv itself, showing concentrated aerial activity around the capital (Source: @kpszsu/65492, @kpszsu/65500, @mon1tor_ua/67867).

Shahed drones were reported heading from Pavlenky toward Poltava and from Ocherka toward Poltava, indicating coordinated strikes on central Ukrainian territories (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67858, @kpszsu/65493).

Shahed drones were observed moving from Lebedyn and Okhtyrka toward Kharkiv, and from Ocherka toward Kharkiv, suggesting a focused northeastern aerial offensive (Source: @kpszsu/65488, @kpszsu/65493).
Ukrainian forces reported a drone attack on an equestrian school in Sumy that destroyed a stable and killed horses, though no human injuries were noted (Source: @UkraineNow/81984).

Russian forces are applying intense pressure around Pokrovsk, Mirnograd and Dobropil, using large numbers of FPV, Lancet, Molniya, Shahed and other drones to support ground advances (Source: @DeepStateUA/23584).
A Russian drone strike on the largest Moscow oil refinery damaged its main processing unit and halted operations, demonstrating reach into Russian heartland (Source: @UkraineNow/82002).
Russian media reported a threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from the Voronezh region, indicating possible long‑range strike capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67830).
Russian authorities denied Ukrainian drone involvement in a reported attack on a bus with Belarusian children in Bryansk, labeling it an information provocation (Source: @UkraineNow/82006).
Shahed drones from Lebedyn and Okhtyrka are moving toward Kharkiv, increasing aerial threat on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/65488).
Russian pressure in Pokrovsk and Mirnograd includes extensive drone usage, aiming to expand control south of Dobropil (Source: @DeepStateUA/23584).
Ukrainian Shahed activity from Pavlenky toward Poltava adds pressure to the central‑eastern axis (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67858).
Russian drone attacks on Zaporizhzhia caused civilian casualties and damage to residential and educational facilities, highlighting eastern vulnerability (Source: @UkraineNow/81983).
Shahed drones were reported moving along the Belarus border into Chernihiv region, indicating northern aerial incursions (Source: @kpszsu/65471).
New groups of attack drones entered Chernihiv from the north, expanding the threat envelope (Source: @kpszsu/65466).
Shahed drones observed over Kyiv from Barishivka and Borispol suggest northern approaches toward the capital (Source: @kpszsu/65492, @kpszsu/65500).
A threat of Iskander‑M missile launches from Voronezh adds a strategic northern‑to‑central strike risk (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67830).
Russian drone pressure around Pokrovsk and Dobropil includes attempts to secure logistics nodes south of Dobropil (Source: @DeepStateUA/23584).
Ukrainian Shahed drones heading toward Poltava from Pavlenky could affect southern approaches to the central front (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67858).
No direct southern front drone incidents were explicitly reported in the source messages.
No additional southern front details were provided.

Shahed attack drones are repeatedly observed over Kyiv, Barishivka, Borispol and other locations, indicating sustained Ukrainian aerial activity (Source: @kpszsu/65492, @kpszsu/65500, @mon1tor_ua/67867).
Russian forces employ a wide variety of drones—FPV, Lancet, Molniya, Shahed, Mavics—across the Pokrovsk‑Dobropil sector (Source: @DeepStateUA/23584).
Ukrainian drones struck Zaporizhzhia, causing civilian casualties and extensive damage to residential and institutional buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81983).
A Russian drone attack on a Moscow oil refinery damaged its main processing unit, showing long‑range strike capability (Source: @UkraineNow/82002).

key_developments: Shahed attack drones are repeatedly observed heading toward Kyiv from Barishivka and Borispol, with multiple Shaheds reported over the capital, indicating concentrated aerial pressure (Source: @kpszsu/65492, @kpszsu/65500, @mon1tor_ua/67867).
Shahed drones are moving toward Poltava from Pavlenky and Ocherka, expanding the threat to central Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67858, @kpszsu/65493).
Shahed drones are advancing toward Kharkiv from Lebedyn, Okhtyrka and Ocherka, suggesting coordinated northeastern strikes (Source: @kpszsu/65488, @kpszsu/65493).
Russian drone attacks on Zaporizhzhia killed one civilian, injured seven, and heavily damaged residential blocks, an office centre and the university (Source: @UkraineNow/81983).
A Russian drone strike on an equestrian school in Sumy destroyed a stable and killed horses, though no human casualties were reported (Source: @UkraineNow/81984).
Russian forces intensify pressure around Pokrovsk, Mirnograd and Dobropil, employing large numbers of various drones to support ground operations (Source: @DeepStateUA/23584).
A threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Voronezh region is reported, raising the risk of long‑range strikes (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67830).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian Shahed drone activity is intensifying around Kyiv, Poltava and Kharkiv, creating a persistent aerial threat to command and control nodes. Russian drone operations around Pokrovsk, Mirnograd and Dobropil, combined with large‑scale drone deployments, aim to erode Ukrainian defensive depth and support ground advances. The reported Iskander‑M missile threat from Voronezh adds a strategic, long‑range dimension that could target critical infrastructure beyond the front lines.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian air defence units are actively tracking and engaging Shahed drones, as indicated by multiple radar reports, but the volume suggests strain on intercept capabilities. Russian forces appear to be leveraging drone swarms to supplement infantry pressure in contested eastern districts, while also extending strike capability into Russian territory, exemplified by the Moscow refinery attack. International partners are increasing air defence aid, as reflected in G7 commitments, to bolster Ukrainian interception capacity.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued monitoring of Shahed launch corridors from Barishivka, Borispol and northern border areas is essential for early warning. The concentration of drone types in the Pokrovsk‑Dobropil sector suggests a coordinated Russian doctrine of mixed UAV employment. The Iskander‑M threat requires integration of missile detection assets in the Voronezh corridor. Ukrainian civilian evacuations in Dnipropetrovsk highlight the broader impact of drone‑induced insecurity on population movements.
